channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Biden ‘deeply saddened’ by Jeju Airlines accident in South Korea
item.description: President Biden said he was “deeply saddened” by the Jeju Airlines accident in South Korea that left nearly 180 people dead.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US investigators to assist South Korea with plane crash investigation
item.description: U.S. investigators are helping South Korea investigate the plane crash on Sunday that killed 179 people on board a plane from Thailand. The team of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Croatia's incumbent president and NATO critic leads in exit poll after presidential election
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s incumbent President Zoran Milanovic has a sweeping lead in Sunday’s election and could win the five-year presidency in the first round, according to an exit poll released immediately after the voting.
item.dc:creator: DARKO BANDIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Flights temporarily suspended at Halifax airport after airplane landing incident
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — An aircraft incident at the Halifax Stanfield International Airport caused temporary delays to all flight operations Saturday night. Airport spokesperson Tiffany Chase said that at approximately 9:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kavelashvili is inaugurated as Georgia's president, complicating its path toward EU
item.description: TBILISI, Goergia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili was formally inaugurated as president of Georgia on Sunday, cementing the ruling party's grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syria's de facto leader says it could take up to 4 years to hold elections
item.description: Syria’s de facto leader has said it could take up to four years to hold elections in Syria, and that he plans on dissolving his Islamist group that led the country’s insurgency at an anticipated national dialogue summit for the country.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kim Jong Un vows to launch 'toughest' anti-US policy
item.description: Kim Jong Un vowed to launch a tough anti-U.S. strategy in the coming years, as President-elect Trump prepares to enter his second term in office. During a five-day Workers’ Party meeting this past week, Kim said North Korea would launch “the toughest anti-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu to undergo major surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will undergo prostate removal surgery on Sunday amid multiple conflicts across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American teacher jailed in Russia is wrongfully detained, State Department formally says
item.description: The State Department said an American teacher arrested in Russian on drug charges in 2021 has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2024 was a year of triumphs and setbacks for Russian leader Vladimir Putin. Here's how it unfolded
item.description: In 2024, President Vladimir Putin further cemented his grip on power and sought to counter Russia's isolation from the West over the war in Ukraine. But he faced continuing challenges, with a deadly attack by gunmen in Moscow and an incursion by Kyiv's troops on his territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel detains the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals during a raid
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's army detained the director of one of northern Gaza 's last functioning hospitals as overnight strikes elsewhere in the territory killed nine people, including children, Palestinian medical officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House: Russia, North Korea treating troops as 'expendable' in conflict with Ukraine
item.description: White House National Security communications adviser John Kirby said Russia was treating North Korean troops as “expendable” by sending them on impractical ground operations in its war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into a crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: President Yoon Suk Yeol off set political crisis in South Korea after imposing martial law. The country's National Assembly has voted to impeach President Han Duck-soo.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government placed sanctions on seven companies on Friday in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory. The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital and multiple ports, while the World Health Organization's director-general said the bombardment occurred nearby as he prepared to board a flight in Sanaa, with a crew member injured.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia being blamed for Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed hundreds of miles off course, killing dozens
item.description: An Azerbaijan Airlines flight from the Azerbaijani capital of Baku to Russia crashed, hospitalizing survivors while dozens are feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation
